text: Lɛ akɔ lɛ atrɔ ní kitɛɛ aʋa a xe akɔ amanɔ ɛ. Akɔ linɔtukpo le aki ɔdzɛʋi yɛ, lɛ yɛ fɛ akɔ lɛ aki yene e.
translated: There was a man who had two wives. The man loved one wife more, and always gave her things.

text: Lɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ onete ebu le onu ki ba mɛ tole si, ‘Ta kutu, ɔʋiaa. Mába wɔ kura. Wɔtutɔ, wɔdimɛ si, wɔawa liwe tile axwɛ ɛ ki liwe le liʋɛ nɛ.
translated: If a person does not respect you and you go to their house, they will say, "Come, child. I was waiting for you. Sit down, let's talk, we will eat food today and food tomorrow too."

translated: But you have a custom that I should release one man for you at the Passover. So do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?” They cried out again, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber. Then Pilate took Jesus and flogged him.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: “Lɛ loso e, mleze ní aŋu plɛplɛ mɛ, lɛ ese linu si mléte liwe lɛ klɔ gi mlɔ Ɔkaɛ aaba a.
translated: When the chief died, his children did not want to bury him, they said they would put him in a room and lock the door.
